questions.q: The Vice-President of India serves as the ex-officio Chairman of which body?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: The Vice-President holds which rank among constitutional offices in India?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President holds the second-highest constitutional office in India.

questions.q: During a vacancy in the office of President, the Vice-President:
questions.explanation: The Vice-President acts as President during a vacancy in that office.

questions.q: What is the term of office of the Vice-President of India?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President holds office for a term of five years.

questions.q: Who was the first Vice-President of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan was the first Vice-President of India, serving from 1952 to 1962.

questions.q: In his capacity as Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, the Vice-President presides over the:
questions.explanation: The Vice-President presides over the Rajya Sabha, the upper house of Parliament.

questions.q: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan, the first Vice-President, later went on to become:
questions.explanation: S. Radhakrishnan served as the first Vice-President and later as the second President of India.

questions.q: The Vice-President is a part of which branch in his role as Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: As presiding officer of the Rajya Sabha, the Vice-President is associated with the legislature.

questions.q: Who administers the oath of office to the Vice-President of India?
questions.explanation: The President administers the oath of office to the Vice-President.

questions.q: Several Vice-Presidents have later been elected to which higher office?
questions.explanation: Several Vice-Presidents, including Radhakrishnan and Zakir Husain, went on to become President.

questions.q: The Vice-President is elected by an electoral college consisting of:
options: Members of both Houses of Parliament
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is elected by the members of both Houses of Parliament assembled at a joint meeting.

questions.q: Which Article of the Constitution provides for a Vice-President of India?
questions.explanation: Article 63 provides that there shall be a Vice-President of India.

questions.q: What is the minimum age to be eligible for election as Vice-President?
questions.explanation: A candidate for Vice-President must have completed 35 years of age.

questions.q: The Vice-President is elected by which method of voting?
options: Proportional representation by single transferable vote
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is elected by proportional representation through the single transferable vote by secret ballot.

questions.q: Unlike the President's electoral college, the Vice-President's electoral college does not include:
options: Elected members of the Lok Sabha
Elected members of the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: The Vice-President's electoral college is confined to Parliament and does not include state legislators.

questions.q: To be qualified for election as Vice-President, a person must be qualified for election as a member of which House?
questions.explanation: A candidate must be qualified for election as a member of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: When the Vice-President acts as President, who performs the duties of Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
options: The Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: While the Vice-President acts as President, the Deputy Chairman performs the duties of Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: A person elected as Vice-President must not hold any other:
questions.explanation: The Vice-President must not hold any office of profit under the Union, state, or any local authority.

questions.q: The provisions relating to the election of the Vice-President are found mainly in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 66 deals with the election of the Vice-President of India.

questions.q: Disputes relating to the election of the Vice-President are decided by:
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court inquires into and decides doubts and disputes arising out of the election of the Vice-President.

questions.q: The Vice-President may resign by writing addressed to the:
questions.explanation: The Vice-President resigns by writing under his hand addressed to the President.

questions.q: A resolution to remove the Vice-President can be initiated only in which House?
questions.explanation: Under Article 67, a resolution to remove the Vice-President can be moved only in the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: A resolution to remove the Vice-President, after being passed by the Rajya Sabha, must be agreed to by the:
questions.explanation: Such a resolution, passed by the Rajya Sabha, must also be agreed to by the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: The removal resolution against the Vice-President requires how much notice before it can be moved?
questions.explanation: At least fourteen days notice is required before a resolution to remove the Vice-President can be moved.

questions.q: The Vice-President may act as President for a maximum period of how long before a new President must be elected?
questions.explanation: When acting as President due to a vacancy, the Vice-President may do so for a maximum of six months, within which a new President must be elected.

questions.q: While acting as President, the Vice-President is entitled to the emoluments of which office?
questions.explanation: While discharging the functions of President, the Vice-President receives the emoluments and privileges of the President, not those of Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: The resolution for the removal of the Vice-President in the Rajya Sabha requires what level of support?
options: A simple majority of those present and voting
A two-thirds majority of total membership
A majority of all the then members of the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: The resolution must be passed by a majority of all the then members of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: The form of oath taken by the Vice-President requires an affirmation to bear true faith and allegiance to the:
questions.explanation: The Vice-President swears to bear true faith and allegiance to the Constitution of India.

questions.q: If the offices of both President and Vice-President fall vacant, who discharges the functions of President?
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: By law, the Chief Justice of India, or in their absence the senior-most Supreme Court judge, discharges the President's functions in such a situation.

questions.q: The salary that the Vice-President draws is in his capacity as:
questions.explanation: The Vice-President draws a salary in his capacity as ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, as the office of Vice-President itself carries no separate salary.
